data:
Process Documentation & Flow Synthesis Report

Finance Function — Single-Site Underground Gold Mining Operation

Consolidated findings from structured interviews across on-site business partners and central finance team · Research basis for automation, RPA, and agentic AI opportunity identification
Executive Summary — At a Glance
Five interviews conducted across two structural layers of a 20-person finance function serving ~600 site employees.
5
Interviews conducted across both teams
14
Process domains in scope
15
Cross-process observations identified
8
Key-person risk flags raised
4
Control gaps identified in AP & payments
1
Process domain with zero coverage (Inventory)
Process maturity is highly variable — transactional processes are well-structured but control-light, while planning, forecasting, and specialist accrual processes carry significant undocumented complexity and concentrated key-person dependency. Excel underpins virtually every non-transactional process, creating fragility throughout the function.
Finance Team Structure
Two-layer split: on-site business partners embedded in operations, and a central finance team covering transactional, reporting, compliance, and planning processes.

Graham Fettes is the sole point of financial governance for the site — overseeing payment authorisation, close sign-off, group reporting, and capex approvals simultaneously. This role concentration is itself a key-person risk at the function level.
Process Domain Coverage Map
Coverage quality reflects depth of interview data available per domain — Full = sufficient to draw complete swim-lane diagram; Partial = key gaps exist; None = no meaningful coverage obtained.

Accounts Payable
Bree (primary) · Graham (payment auth)

9 of 14 domains have full or near-full coverage. The Inventory & Stores Finance Interface domain has no coverage and should be prioritised in a follow-up interview round, likely with a warehouse or stores manager.
Cross-Process Observations — Analysis
15 observations surfaced across interviews, categorised by their primary nature. Many observations carry multiple tags; primary classification is used here.
Observations by Category
Count of cross-process observations per primary classification
Key-person risk is the dominant observation category — reflecting a finance function that has concentrated critical institutional knowledge in a small number of individuals without documentation or succession mechanisms.
Key-Person Risk by Individual
Number of undocumented or non-transferable process dependencies per person
Kobus van der Merwe carries the highest single-person risk profile — his standing data file and revenue accrual methodology are the most material undocumented assets in the finance function.
Cross-Process Observations — Full Register
All 15 observations sourced from interview captures, categorised and tagged for use in subsequent automation and improvement analysis.
# Observation Affected Processes Nature Source
1 Pronto cost centre misalignment — both BPs maintain Excel remapping layers to bridge the gap between GL design and operational reality Cost Accounting · Management Reporting · Budgeting Workaround Dale · Kobus
2 Spreadsheet-as-system dependency — critical processes rely on individually maintained Excel files with no version control, access management, or audit trail All planning, reporting & accrual processes Key-person risk All interviewees
3 Revenue accrual — Kobus sole owner — doré assay application, silver credit, treatment charge logic, and reversal methodology exist only in his head and partially in the standing data file AR & Revenue · Month-End Close · Management Reporting Key-person risk Kobus · Graham
4 Reforecast model — Graham sole owner — master consolidation model is undocumented; internal consistency check relies entirely on 9 years of operational familiarity; risk registered but mitigation not executed Budgeting & Forecasting · Management Reporting Key-person risk Graham
5 Depreciation module — Sarah sole operator — only person who understands Pronto's depreciation configuration; close sequencing logic also lives in her head Month-End Close · Fixed Assets Key-person risk Sarah
6 Journal submission rework ~30–40% — incomplete submissions (missing GL, cost centre, description, or wrong period) returned for rework; can consume a full day of delay in a 5-day close window Month-End Close Manual effort Sarah
7 Static copy-paste reporting assembly — management pack built by exporting Pronto to Excel, formatting, then copy-pasting into Word; any correction requires full re-extraction Management Reporting Manual effort Sarah · Graham
8 Provisional revenue reversal noise — 4–5 reversals/year create unexplained volatility in reported revenue line visible to Group CFO; reversal logic undocumented AR & Revenue · Month-End Close · Management Reporting Data issue Kobus · Sarah
9 Unannounced cost reallocations — central finance reallocates costs between production cost centres without notifying the relevant BP; discovered at month-end reconciliation Cost Accounting · Management Reporting · Business Partnering Data issue Dale
10 No SoD on non-PO invoice approval — Bree both keys and approves non-PO invoices; no second reviewer before invoices enter payment-eligible queue Accounts Payable Control gap Bree · Graham
11 Single payment authoriser — Graham is sole banking platform authoriser; review depth is volume-dependent and acknowledged as less thorough during close week Cash Management · Accounts Payable Control gap Graham · Bree
12 AP exceptions tracked outside Pronto — parallel Excel tracker maintained because Pronto status field doesn't support exception workflow; if lost, exception visibility disappears entirely Accounts Payable · Procurement & PO Workaround Bree
13 No centralised capex document repository — approval trails exist only in email inboxes; no single source of truth for submission status or approval version history Fixed Assets & CapEx · Business Partnering Workaround Graham · Kobus
14 Standing data file — single point of failure — Kobus's Excel file (cost centre mappings, unit rates, consumption rates) underpins all plant financial processes; no backup or second maintainer Cost Accounting · AR & Revenue · Budgeting · Fixed Assets Key-person risk Kobus
15 Manual remittance advice distribution — ~1 hour per run, twice weekly (~100 hrs/year); vendor email addresses fragmented across Pronto and personal contact file; high volume, low judgement, no evident reason to remain manual Accounts Payable Manual effort Bree
Key-Person Risk Profiles
Undocumented or non-transferable process dependencies concentrated in specific individuals. No mitigation has been executed for the most severe concentrations.

"Work has calcified around how I do it, not how it should be done." — Kobus van der Merwe, BP Plant Processing (self-described observation on key-person risk)
Systems & Tools Landscape
Six platforms span all 14 process domains. No document management system is in use — the shared network drive serves this function informally.
Pronto (ERP)
Primary ERP system. Used for transactional processing, GL, fixed assets, depreciation, and cost centre reporting. Cost centre structure misaligned with operational reporting needs.
Accounts Payable GL / Journals Fixed Assets Depreciation Cost Centre Reporting Financial Reporting
Microsoft Excel
Pervasive across ALL planning, reporting, and accrual processes. Acts as the de-facto planning system, workflow tool, and control mechanism throughout the function. High fragility.
Weekly Scorecards Revenue Accrual Budget / Reforecast Management Pack Journal Log AP Exceptions CapEx Tracker Standing Data Close Calendar
Microsoft Word
Used for management pack narrative sections, weekly scorecard summaries (Dale), and business case documents. No live links to Excel data — all content is manually copy-pasted.
Management Reporting Weekly Scorecard Business Cases
Email (Outlook / Shared Mailbox)
Primary workflow and handoff mechanism across the entire function. apinvoices@site.com.au is the AP invoice intake channel. Close coordination, journal submissions, remittances, and group reporting all flow through email.
AP Invoice Receipt Journal Submissions Close Coordination Payment File Transfer Remittances Group Reporting
Online Banking Platform
Used exclusively by Graham Fettes for payment file upload and release. AP team prepares the file in Excel; Graham releases it. Single authoriser — no second approval required.
Payment Authorisation Cash Management
Shared Network Drive
Serves as the de-facto document management system. Used for AP invoice filing, audit working papers, Met team production tracking file, Kobus's standing data file, and budget models. No version control or access management.
AP Filing Audit Working Papers Budget Models Production Tracking Standing Data
The systems landscape is characterised by a single ERP (Pronto) that is structurally misaligned with operational needs, surrounded by an unmanaged layer of Excel files that carry the real intelligence of the function. This creates significant fragility, audit risk, and automation complexity.
